items.label:
Yesterday I visited the Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek and saw this ancient oil lamp.

More than 3000 years old… and somehow, th
items.summary:
Yesterday I visited the Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek and saw this ancient oil lamp.

More than 3000 years old… and somehow, the design still feels contemporary.
Functional. Essential. Timeless.

It’s impressive to realize that some objects were designed so well that humanity still uses the same logic thousands of years later.
You can find surprisingly similar designs today, even on Amazon. 😨 

It made me think about design in a different way:
not creating for trends, but creating with the ambition to

items.label:
This cover has been on my mind.
No logo.
No band name.
No title.
Nothing.
Just a cow, standing there, looking at you.

A
items.summary:
This cover has been on my mind.
No logo.
No band name.
No title.
Nothing.
Just a cow, standing there, looking at you.

And the question is: what kind of confidence do you need, as a band, to do something like this?

Not just removing all branding but choosing an image that feels almost… meaningless.

Why a cow? Exactly.
Because it doesn’t need to make sense.
This is where design stops, and art begins.
Design explains. Art doesn’t have to.

And yet, it’s often art the irrational, the unclear, the

items.label: LEGO Smart Play x LEGO Star Wars - Official Trailer
items.summary:
Yesterday I met Blinkink, the studio behind this the LEGO Group Smart Play X LEGO Star Wars film and it genuinely inspired me.

In a moment where #AI is reshaping everything, seeing this level of craft, storytelling, and production hits differently.

A simple unboxing becomes a portal.
A kid’s room turns into a cinematic world.
Reality and imagination blur and for a moment, you don’t just imagine… you live it.

That’s the real product: not the toy, but the ability to bring your imagination to li

metadata.fullTitle: LEGO Smart Play x LEGO Star Wars - Official Trailer

items.label: Beauty Is Not Enough
items.summary:
This happened in 2022.
I was traveling alone through Colombia for two months.
No strict plan. Just movement.

When I arrived in Santa Marta, I decided to hike to the Lost City.
Four days.
60 kilometers.
1,300 meters of elevation gain.

I thought it would be just another beautiful experience.
It wasn’t.

Somewhere in the jungle, walking side by side with the guide’s translator, he told me something I’ll never forget.

He didn’t own his first pair of shoes until he was fourteen.
He also told me he

metadata.fullTitle: Beauty Is Not Enough

items.summary:
In January last year, I started my journey at LEGO Group as a Brand Designer.

Smart Play was the very first project I got involved in.

The project was already in progress when I joined, so I stepped in somewhere in the middle of the process, trying to contribute where I could and learn as much as possible along the way.

It was incredibly exciting and at the same time completely confidential. I couldn’t talk about it with anyone.

I had the chance to listen, collaborate, and share small inputs

items.label: #visceralpoems #escribujos #creativity #art #design #visualpoetry #mindfulness
items.summary:
A few years ago, during a five-month journey across Latin America, I started collecting words.

Not objects. Not souvenirs. Words.

Words from songs, books, conversations, and moments of silence. Words that helped me grow, heal, and remember who I wanted to become.

When I returned home, I wanted to keep those ideas close. But I didn’t want to fill my walls with quotes that would eventually disappear into the background.

So I started merging writing and drawing.

The words became hidden inside

metadata.fullTitle: #visceralpoems #escribujos #creativity #art #design #visualpoetry #mindfulness

Today I received a package LEGO x Crocs.

And honestly… these are my first C
items.summary:
Working at the LEGO Group also means this:

Today I received a package LEGO x Crocs.

And honestly… these are my first Crocs ever.

I would have never imagined myself buying a pair o Crocs.
But these? These, yes. Of course. Straight into my house.

That’s the strange power of the LEGO Group .

It makes you smile before you even open the box.
It changes your face for a second.
You look at it like a kid would.

And maybe that’s one of the most beautiful things about this brand: it reminds adults t

items.label:
I’ve been wearing armor for a long time.

Good armor.

Beautiful armor.

The kind that looks like confidence.

On June 1
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on June 23, 2026: "I’ve been wearing armor for a long time.

Good armor.

Beautiful armor.

The kind that looks like confidence.

On June 19, I took it off.

I invited the people I love to my rooftop.

White clothes.

Your favorite drink.

No agenda.

Live music.

A sunset over Copenhagen.

But none of that was the point.

What I really wanted was something much simpler:

To let the people closest to me meet the man living inside the armor.

That’s the only birthday gift I wished for

But recently I realiz
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on May 26, 2026: "I’ve been drawing for years.
And for a long time, I thought the goal was to improve my technique.

But recently I realized something.

The most important thing is not improving the drawing.
It’s improving myself.

Your art is like your body.
What comes out depends on what you consume.

Like shit.
What you shit is what you eat.

So instead of obsessing over technique, I want to start paying attention to what feeds my mind, my eyes, my soul, my habits, my silence, my

items.label:
Kissing is an art. 

A kiss is one of the most sensual things that exists. 
Not everyone knows how to kiss. 
Not everyon
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on May 11, 2026: "Kissing is an art. 

A kiss is one of the most sensual things that exists. 
Not everyone knows how to kiss. 
Not everyone knows how to truly enjoy a kiss. 

But when you find someone who does… 
and you both feel it at the same time… 
that moment of “wait… you feel this too?” 

That’s something extraordinary.
#kiss #kissing #kissingisanart".

items.label:
When I started @urees__ I didn’t just want to make clothes.
I wanted to rescue stories.

Every pair I find has lived a l
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on November 28, 2025: "When I started @urees__ I didn’t just want to make clothes.
I wanted to rescue stories.

Every pair I find has lived a life before mine:
walked through cities I’ve never seen,
danced through nights I can’t remember,
been loved, torn, repaired…
and started again.

I take all those pieces the scars, the chapters
and fuse them into something new.

Not perfect. Not uniform.
But alive.

Because beauty isn’t about looking new,
it’s about carrying history with dignity

Not mine alone but one I’m proud to have contributed to.

Some works don’t leave you.
It wasn’t a “comfortable” project.

items.label:
Do you know how many times in the past
I didn’t know how to act,
or which part of myself to show
depending on the person
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on March 7, 2026: "Do you know how many times in the past
I didn’t know how to act,
or which part of myself to show
depending on the person or situation in front of me?

It’s human to empathize,
to listen,
to adapt.

But real freedom arrives
when you stop trying to control it all
and simply embrace all that you are.

If you find yourself on this path in life, these flowers are for you.".

This year didn’t shout.
It whispered.

I changed places,

items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on December 23, 2025: "2025

This year didn’t shout.
It whispered.

I changed places,
but more than that,
I changed skin.

After 30 years of fighting my own body,
it finally softened.
The psoriasis is gone.
And with it, a silent war
I thought I had to carry forever.

I learned that healing doesn’t arrive with fireworks.
It arrives quietly,
when you stop resisting
and start listening.

I met incredible people.
Souls that crossed my path
exactly when I needed them.
Some stayed for a mo

items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on February 25, 2026: "An eye that doesn’t blink.

Sometimes we don’t realize how many good things we already have 
things we should be grateful for.

We don’t need to ask to be blessed.
We already are.

Blessed by the life we have.
Blessed by the chance to see, to feel, to experience.

Stay awake.
Don’t blink.

Be curious.
Enjoy it.".

items.label:
Sometimes I forget about myself.

I forget that energy isn’t only what moves my body it’s also what feeds my spirit.

I
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on October 30, 2025: "Sometimes I forget about myself.

I forget that energy isn’t only what moves my body it’s also what feeds my spirit.

I rush through life, letting the world enter unfiltered into my thoughts, my space, my heart.

This drawing is a reminder.
A way to tell myself to protect my energy physical, spiritual.

Because it’s not selfishness, it’s respect.

It’s saying no to what drains me, and yes to what makes me vibrate.

Keep your energy safe.
Always.

 #visceralpoems

items.label: When I first read LEGO’s story, I imagined that very first model consumed by flames, probably for the third time in just
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on August 9, 2025: "When I first read LEGO’s story, I imagined that very first model consumed by flames, probably for the third time in just a few years.
It must have been heartbreaking… watching everything turn to ashes. LEGO’s factory burned down three times in its early days. 

But from that third fire, the brand was forged forever into one of the most revolutionary in the world.

Never give up even when it feels like it’s truly the end. ❤️".

Three years ago, during a long journe
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on September 10, 2025: "Sometimes, the only way I can breathe is by writing and drawing at the same time.

Three years ago, during a long journey through Latin America, I started carrying a small notebook with me. In it, I poured out words that weren’t just words they twisted, danced, and transformed into shapes. They became escribujos: poems that look like drawings, drawings that feel like poems.

That notebook saved me. It gave me a place to let my emotions bleed, to turn pain into

items.label:
During my time in Mexico, I did something that changed me forever.
A Vipassana meditation retreat: 10 days in silence.
N
items.summary:
fiogiuseppe on July 1, 2025: "During my time in Mexico, I did something that changed me forever.
A Vipassana meditation retreat: 10 days in silence.
No phone. No talking. No eye contact.
Just meditation. 12 hours a day.
Wake-up at 4am. Two meals. No distractions.
No escape. Just me, with myself.

At first, it was brutal.
Then something started to shift.
I began eating slowly.
Washing myself with care.
Putting on lotion like my body was sacred.
I remembered that I too deserve love.

During the br

Ideas that once lived inside my head.

If you place all these pieces on a table,

A patchwork of art and design,

Nothing came fast.

Time matters but so does showing up.

Creating means trying.
Trying means failing.
And failing means learning how to accept yourself
exactly as you are imperfect, unfinished, alive.
